description_long: This object is a solar energy infrastructure installation featuring a set of rectangular, dark-colored photovoltaic panels mounted on a low, flat platform. The panels are arranged in a compact, grid-like configuration, with visible support structures beneath them, likely made of metal. The platform includes some red and gray components, possibly representing electrical boxes or mounting hardware, and the overall structure appears robust and industrial. The panels are slightly elevated above the base, allowing for airflow and maintenance access. No visible cabling or inverter units are apparent from the exterior views. The design suggests it is intended for ground installation in an urban environment, such as a parking lot, park, or rooftop.
description: A compact ground-mounted solar panel array with a sturdy metal frame and several rectangular photovoltaic panels arranged in a grid. The structure features red and gray support elements and is designed for urban solar energy generation.
description_view_0: From this side view, the low profile of the solar array and the elevation of the panels above the base are visible.
description_view_1: This view shows the front of the array, highlighting the grid arrangement of the panels and the red and gray support components.
description_view_2: The opposite side view emphasizes the length of the array and the alignment of the panels along the platform.
description_view_3: From the rear, the support structure and the back sides of the panels are visible, along with the arrangement of the mounting hardware.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 10-15 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a metal (likely aluminum or steel) frame
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 3-4 rectangular panels arranged side by side
